objective:
FICHE_STRATEGIE v1
id: mean_reversion_bollinger_rsi
archetype: mean_reversion_bollinger_rsi
family: mean_reversion
timeframe: 1h
side: both
indicators:
- bollinger(period=40, std_dev=2.9)
- rsi(period=10)
- atr(period=14)
entry:
- long: close < bollinger.lower and rsi < 35
- short: close > bollinger.upper and rsi > 80
exit:
- condition: close crosses bollinger.middle or rsi crosses 50
risk:
stop_atr_mult: 2.0
tp_atr_mult: 2.5
description: ATR stop-loss (2.0x) and take-profit (2.5x)
constraints:
- no_lookahead: true
- only_registry_indicators: true

iterations.hypothesis: The Bollinger Bands and RSI indicators can be combined to identify mean reversion trading opportunities by triggering buy signals when the price crosses below the lower band and sell signals when it crosses above the upper band, while using a trailing stop-loss based on ATR and taking profits at specific levels.
Combining Bollinger Bands and RSI can provide a more robust entry signal in mean reversion trading strategy.

iterations.hypothesis: Using the Bollinger Bands and Relative Strength Index (RSI) to identify overbought/oversold conditions followed by a long entry when the price closes below the lower band, and short entry when the price crosses above the upper band. Additionally, using the Average True Range (ATR) to set risk management parameters, with an ATR stop-loss and multiple target levels for take profits.
